items.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament

children.content: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.

articles.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

children.content: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

image.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

gallery.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie look on at a woman wearing dark top, the man is also seen in a camera viewfinder

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie look on at a woman wearing dark top, the man is also seen in a camera viewfinder

items.title: Plagued by 18 months of blackouts, locals say $10m fix is long overdue

children.content: After losing hundreds of thousands of dollars due to power outages, Yorke Peninsula businesses say SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan should have come a long time ago.

image.altText: A man in a grey hoodie standing behind beer taps.

items.title: Fracking could open the door to 10 years of gas supply but is there a risk?

children.content: A decade's worth of gas supply for South Australia is potentially sitting below the Limestone Coast region. But will it ever be extracted?

image.altText: A farmer holds an anti mining yellow sign by a gate on a farm

items.title: Caution and long-term thinking as budget adds to softening real estate market

children.content: After riding a post-COVID wave of booming prices, real estate agents say SA's housing market is starting to level out, as federal budget tax changes and rising interest rates reduce buyer appetite.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a brown suit gesticulates at a banner
A man wearing a brown coat speaks while holding a brochure while another man stands behind and a house behind that

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a brown suit gesticulates at a banner
A man wearing a brown coat speaks while holding a brochure while another man stands behind and a house behind that

items.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare

children.content: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

image.altText: An AFL player in black and white stripes is carried off the field on the shoulders of two others. Camera flash to the left

items.title: Collingwood champion's achievement reopens old wound outside Victoria

children.content: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

image.altText: Craig Bradley moves the ball forward for South Australia.

items.title: Why there's more talk of an AUKUS 'plan B'

children.content: Defence industry experts argue Australia should consider how to prevent a naval "capability gap" — or risk being left exposed.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry

children.content: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

image.altText: Close up of an agave plant.

items.title: Little penguin numbers climb on Kangaroo Island despite algal bloom impact

children.content: Little penguin numbers on Kangaroo Island have rebounded, but estimates show there are only about 700 adult breeders after a harmful algal bloom.

gallery.altText: A little penguin peers out of a rocky burrow

thumbnail.altText: A little penguin peers out of a rocky burrow

items.title: Boat driver jailed after running over swimmer who suffered 'horrific injuries'

children.content: A woman who was likely more than three times the legal blood alcohol limit when she ran over a swimmer while driving a boat has been jailed for more than two years.

image.altText: A blonde woman wearing large sunglasses and smiling

items.title: Doctors push for e-bike ban in SA for riders aged under 16

children.content: South Australia's peak medical body is calling for reforms to current e-bike laws in the state, including a ban on children aged under 16 from riding them.

image.altText: E bikes lined up in a row inside a bike shop

items.title: 36ers eye dream team as former Perth coach reunites with Bryce Cotton

children.content: One of Australian basketball's most successful coaches and one of the national league's greatest players have paired in Adelaide.

image.altText: A composite image of two men

items.title: Why consumers are forking out more for whiting, calamari and garfish

children.content: South Australia's peak seafood body says a short supply of some species due to the algal bloom and commercial fishing restrictions has led to slight price increases, as it warns it could take industry five to 10 years to fully recover.

image.altText: Fillets for sale at a fishmonger at the Adelaide Central Market.

items.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat as travellers stay close to home

children.content: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.

image.altText: RVs at a caravan park

items.title: 'An absolutely pleasant surprise': Why single mums are moving in together

children.content: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.

image.altText: Hayly Grimmond and Anja Homburg and their children smile while siting on a bed.

items.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question

children.content: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

gallery.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

thumbnail.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

items.title: Five economists share opinions on Labor's budget and Coalition's reply

children.content: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."

image.altText: A man in a suit stands at a podium in parliament.

items.title: This private island paradise has a bargain price tag, and problems galore

children.content: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.

image.altText: An island, seen from above

items.title: Trump revives centuries-old doctrine that shaped US-Cuba relations

children.content: The Monroe Doctrine was once a core tenet of US foreign policy. US President Donald Trump has brought it back with a new interpretation.

image.altText: Trump gives a thumbs up.

items.title: As a kid, Thitiwoot wanted to name a dino. He never knew it'd be this big

children.content: It started out as a bone sticking out of the mud next to a lake in Thailand — and now scientists say it's the biggest dinosaur ever found in South-East Asia.

gallery.altText: An enormous brown bone on its end stands slightly taller than a Thai man next to it wearing all black and sneakers.
Diagram of a long-necked dinosaur skeleton in black, with a human for scale, smaller than its legs. A few bones are yellow.
Wide shot of two men in a store room with shelves behind them, one holding a bone and the other flicking through a book.

thumbnail.altText: An enormous brown bone on its end stands slightly taller than a Thai man next to it wearing all black and sneakers.
Diagram of a long-necked dinosaur skeleton in black, with a human for scale, smaller than its legs. A few bones are yellow.
Wide shot of two men in a store room with shelves behind them, one holding a bone and the other flicking through a book.

items.title: Auckland makes A-League history with grand-final triumph over Sydney

children.content: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

image.altText: A soccer player in blue and black screams in celebration with floodlight behind him

items.title: Jamie-Lee Price thinks fake tan could solve a lot of netball's problems

children.content: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.

items.title: Meet the women turning bicycle tyres and timber offcuts into furniture

children.content: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a woman at a work bench constructing wooden furniture.

items.title: Put away the dictionary. It's spelling bee time!

children.content: Know your way around a homonym? Nah, me neither but come in and have a crack at our Sunday quiz anyway.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Iran war lays bare overlooked holes in Australia's sovereign capability

children.content: The Iran war has not only highlighted the vulnerability of global supply chains — it has also exposed the overlooked parts of Australia's manufacturing sector needed in a crisis.

gallery.altText: An assembly line at a meatworks, workers in head to toe PPE are cutting lamb and completing other tasks

thumbnail.altText: An assembly line at a meatworks, workers in head to toe PPE are cutting lamb and completing other tasks
